checks.a_analysis: The document's content is mapped one-to-one from the source PDF to the JSON output. Each instructional text block and the title appear exactly once, in a sequence that mirrors the original document's flow. There are no instances of duplicated paragraphs, sections, or images.

The output contains a single heading for the main title, assigned level 2. While H1 is conventional for document titles, using H2 for the only heading present does not violate hierarchical rules as no levels are skipped. This is a valid structural normalization.
The source document does not contain any tables; therefore, there is no table structure to evaluate or corruption to report.

The JSON preserves the intended step-by-step reading order of the guide across all three pages. The progression from initial setup to connection success follows the same sequence found in the source PDF.

The source document presents its instructions as discrete paragraphs rather than formal lists. The output correctly captures these as paragraph elements, maintaining the meaning and flow without incorrectly forcing a list structure.
There are no hyperlinks or visible URLs in the source document. The output correctly contains no links, avoiding any fabricated or injected content.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output follows the same sequential layout as the source PDF, presenting text instructions followed by the corresponding visual aids. While many images are missing (see V3), the overall organizational structure and visual flow of the information are maintained.
The document does not contain any tables.

Seven out of the nine screenshots provided in the source PDF are missing from the HTML rendering. The first two images (the system tray icons and the quick settings menu) are present, but all subsequent images essential for following the guide (network selection, connection prompt, password entry, verification status, splash page, and success screen) are replaced by green placeholder boxes.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF contains meaningful screenshots of the Windows UI and login portals on pages 1, 2, and 3 to guide the user.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot shows large green rectangular placeholder blocks instead of images for all steps starting from 'Select NCGov-Guest' to the end of the document.
checks.b_evidence_location: HTML screenshots, following every text instruction block from 'Select NCGov-Guest' onwards.

checks.a_analysis: No body content, paragraphs, or images are duplicated in the output.
